title: Rumor control board
summary: A board that separates confirmed instructions, unverified reports, questions, and actions so panic does not spread.
content: Rumors fill silence. A rumor control board gives fear a place to be checked without shaming people. Use four columns: confirmed, unverified, questions, actions. Every item needs time and source, and old disproved items should be archived.
steps: Create confirmed, unverified, questions, and actions columns.
Write source and time for every claim.
Move items only after confirmation or rejection.
warnings: Do not post private accusations or names without need.
Do not punish people for reporting uncertainty.
common_mistakes: Leaving frightening rumors visible after disproval.
Letting the loudest person decide truth.
alternatives: Run a smaller version of Rumor control board with one responsible person, one helper, and one visible paper note.
Use a safer fallback location or method for Rumor control board if the first choice becomes wet, crowded, contaminated, dark, unstable, or unsafe.
Pause Rumor control board and verify the written record with a second person before moving scarce supplies.
failure_signs: People cannot explain who is responsible for Rumor control board or when the next check happens.
Supplies connected to Rumor control board are unlabelled, damp, contaminated, damaged, missing, or being used for the wrong purpose.
The routine ignores clear records, verified messages, offline copies, and review times and starts creating new risk instead of reducing existing risk during “Rumor control board”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Rumor control board as a reason to delay evacuation, emergency calls, medical care, fire response, or official local instructions.
Do not use Rumor control board when the location is structurally unsafe, chemically suspicious, smoky, flooded, violent, or otherwise immediately dangerous.
Do not improvise hazardous repairs, dosing, chemical handling, weapon use, or electrical/gas work under the label of Rumor control board.
short_term: For the first hours of Rumor control board, keep the action small, visible, and reversible: write the goal, assign one owner, protect vulnerable people, separate safe from unsafe supplies, and set a review time.
long_term: If Rumor control board remains necessary for days or weeks, rotate roles, preserve written lessons, replace fragile supplies, and train another person so the group does not depend on one expert.
if_method_fails: If Rumor control board fails, stop escalation, move to the safest fallback, record what failed, protect people first, and restart only with a simpler written plan.
environment_notes: Adapt Rumor control board to the real environment: check clear records, verified messages, offline copies, and review times, local weather, building condition, water access, light, noise, animals, neighbours, and safe exits before repeating the routine.
